---
title: "ai_responses"
format: html
editor: visual
---



how do I get multiple things on a label in ggplotly?

The trick is: ggplotly only shows one “tooltip field” at a time, so if you want multiple things, you must combine them into one string first.


p <- ggplot(data, aes(
  x = profit_men,
  y = W,
  text = paste(
    "Team:", TEAM,
    "<br>Year:", YEAR,
    "<br>Wins:", W
  )
)) +
  geom_point()

plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")




How do an If else statement for a shiny app when I have some variables that are not numeric?


In Shiny, if/else works the same as regular R—but you use it inside reactive contexts (like renderPlot, reactive, etc.), not at the top level.



output$plot <- renderPlot({

  if (input$variable == "W") {
    ggplot(data, aes(x = W)) + geom_histogram()
  } else {
    ggplot(data, aes(x = profit_men)) + geom_histogram()
  }

})



How to use multiple labels on ggplotly?

ggplotly does NOT use label the way you expect—it often just prints the expression (so you see "paste(...)").


aes(text = paste(
  "Team:", TEAM,
  "<br>Year:", YEAR,
  "<br>Conf:", CONF
